content.full_text:
1 
Act. 
must 
our 
bee: 
Jeanne 
Special Compliance Representative 


April 22, 1976 
Fair Political Practices Commission 
1100 K Street 
Sacramento, CA 
95814 
Attention: 
Technical Assistance and Analysis Division 
Gentlemen: 
Recently our office has been confronted with the following two problems 
which are similar in nature. 
First, a candidate in a local city election received contributions during 
her campaign which were duly and properly reported. 
However, now the 
candidate is holding excess funds left over from that campaign, and does 
not intend to engage in any future campaigns. 
The funds are presently 
being held in a separate account. 
Our question is, what is the proper 
method to dispose of these funds? 
May they be contributed to charity? 
The second problem arose in a similar situation involving the same local 
city election, only the excess funds are held by the Treasurer of a campaign 
committee. 
The same question has arisen as to the proper disposition of 
these funds held by the Treasurer of the campaign committee. 
The funds held in both situations are a result of small contributions which 
were not required to be reported on an individual basis, and therefore the 
source of the funds is not known so as to allow return of the funds to the 
individuals who contributed them. 
We are not aware of any particular provlslon of the Political Reform Act 
which imposes any requirements or gives any directions for the disposition 
of the funds in question. 
We will appreciate any information or advice available from your office. 
Very truly yours, 
Assistant City Attorney 
SN:sl:Th:5 
cc: 
W.E.

sections.question: What is the proper method to dispose of excess campaign funds held by a candidate who does not intend to engage in any future campaigns? May they be contributed to charity? How should excess funds held by a campaign committee treasurer be properly disposed of when the source of the funds is unknown and cannot be returned to individual contributors?
sections.question_synthetic: The questioner seeks guidance on the proper disposition of excess campaign funds in two situations: funds held by a candidate and funds held by a campaign committee treasurer, where the original contributors cannot be identified and the funds were not individually reported.
sections.parsing_notes: No section headers found; LLM: This is an incoming request letter from an Assistant City Attorney, not an FPPC response. The document contains a QUESTION section but no CONCLUSION section. The letter presents two related scenarios regarding campaign fund disposition.

May they be contributed to charity? How should excess funds held by a campaign committee treasurer be properly disposed of when the source of the funds is unknown and cannot be returned to individual contributors?

embedding.summary: An Assistant City Attorney requests FPPC guidance on the proper disposition of excess campaign funds from a local city election in two situations: funds held by a candidate not planning future campaigns and funds held by a campaign committee treasurer, where the original contributors are unknown.
